adjustments.summary: Phase 6 should validate the corrected sandbox payment routes, hosted-checkout return behavior, verification-error fallback, and manual-bank review flow under a fresh hardening pass.
Do not enable canonical posting for the new payment flow until an operator-approved account mapping exists for the legacy transactions surface.
Keep live shurjoPay activation, live merchant-panel changes, and the current WordPress IPN cutover out of scope until explicit approval is recorded.
Do not extend online payments to donor flows until a dedicated donor payable model exists.

If shurjoPay later supplies a distinct callback signature secret, authoritative provider event identifier, or updated return/callback contract, review and integrate them during controlled hardening or go-live work rather than changing the sandbox scaffold ad hoc.

notes: Phase 5 is partially complete but sandbox ready for guardian invoice payments with documented limits.
The current implementation uses verify-API confirmation instead of an undocumented callback signature scheme.

Closeout hardening aligned the HFS order-prefix default, forwarded the fail return URL, and converted verification transport/auth errors into manual-review outcomes instead of raw browser/IPN exceptions.
The next thread should focus on hardening and final verification, not on live cutover.
